channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US-Iran Talks Begin In Switzerland

media:content.media:description: US-Iran Peace Talks Live Updates

item.description: US-Iran Peace Talks Live Updates: JD Vance had on Thursday cancelled his trip to Switzerland amid rising tension in Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran Peace Talks Live Updates: JD Vance had on Thursday cancelled his trip to Switzerland amid rising tension in Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

item.title: Paraglider Dangles 60 Metres In Air After Getting Stuck On Crane In China

media:content.media:description: The glider's lines were caught in the jib of the crane.

item.description: The terrifying moment was captured on camera and quickly spread online, highlighting how quickly an adventure flight can turn dangerous.
item.content:encoded: The terrifying moment was captured on camera and quickly spread online, highlighting how quickly an adventure flight can turn dangerous.

item.title: JD Vance, Iranian Officials Arrive In Switzerland To Begin Talks

media:content.media:description: Iran's main focus during negotiations on Sunday will be the ongoing war between Israel and Lebanon.

item.description: The framework was signed last week, and now top US and Iranian negotiators are in a 60-day sprint to reach an agreement on the technical details that hold massive implications for the world economy and global security.
item.content:encoded: The framework was signed last week, and now top US and Iranian negotiators are in a 60-day sprint to reach an agreement on the technical details that hold massive implications for the world economy and global security.

item.title: Malaysia First, China Next: Tarique Rahman Embarks On Debut Foreign Tour

media:content.media:description: After completing his visit to Malaysia, Rahman will travel to China.

item.description: Rahman left Dhaka on a two-day official visit to Malaysia before proceeding to China to attend the 17th Annual Meeting of the New Champions of the World Economic Forum (WEF), popularly known as the Summer Davos Forum.
item.content:encoded: Rahman left Dhaka on a two-day official visit to Malaysia before proceeding to China to attend the 17th Annual Meeting of the New Champions of the World Economic Forum (WEF), popularly known as the Summer Davos Forum.

item.title: Ray-Ban Heir Escalates Fight For Control Of Family's $11.5 Billion Fortune

media:content.media:description: Leonardo Maria Del Vecchio is trying to be the largest shareholder

item.description: Del Vecchio is attempting to buy the combined 25% stakes held by his siblings Luca and Paola in Delfin.
item.content:encoded: Del Vecchio is attempting to buy the combined 25% stakes held by his siblings Luca and Paola in Delfin.

item.title: UK PM Keir Starmer May Quit Next Week, Top Contenders Who Could Replace Him

media:content.media:description: The top contenders who could replace Keir Starmer

item.description: The speculation about his exit has grown after former Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham won the Makerfield by-election, defeating Reform UK's Robert Kenyon by more than 9,000 votes.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1116180/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>The speculation about his exit has grown after former Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham won the Makerfield by-election, defeating Reform UK's Robert Kenyon by more than 9,000 votes.

item.title: "F**k. It's Big A** Dog": Video Shows US Cops Shooting Pet After 911 Call

media:content.media:description: The incident occurred on June 13

item.description: The Los Angeles Police Department had received a radio call from a woman who said that her neighbour had been "screaming 'Oh my God' for 20 minutes".
item.content:encoded: The Los Angeles Police Department had received a radio call from a woman who said that her neighbour had been "screaming 'Oh my God' for 20 minutes".

item.title: French Police Raid House For Drugs And Discover Picasso Painting

media:content.media:description: The original search took place on Monday in Champigny-sur-Marne.

item.description: A routine police raid targeting drug trafficking in a Paris suburb unexpectedly uncovered an authenticated painting by Pablo Picasso alongside cannabis, cash, and luxury goods, leading to court appearances for four individuals and a new investigation into stolen property.
item.content:encoded: A routine police raid targeting drug trafficking in a Paris suburb unexpectedly uncovered an authenticated painting by Pablo Picasso alongside cannabis, cash, and luxury goods, leading to court appearances for four individuals and a new investigation into stolen property.

item.title: Students Sexually Abused, Forced To Dig Graves, Eat Vomit At New York Boarding School

media:content.media:description: Representational Image

item.description: The lawsuit claims that students who tried to escape were tracked down and brought back using K9 dogs.
item.content:encoded: The lawsuit claims that students who tried to escape were tracked down and brought back using K9 dogs.

item.title: Meloni's Spat With Trump Shows Italy PM's Readiness To Risk A Bigger Fight

media:content.media:description: Meloni said Trump lies and panders to enemies while turning on his friends

item.description: Giorgia Meloni, the person who Donald Trump once praised for taking "Europe by storm", has done three things that some US allies may have thought privately but never said publicly.
item.content:encoded: Giorgia Meloni, the person who Donald Trump once praised for taking "Europe by storm", has done three things that some US allies may have thought privately but never said publicly.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Key Oil Refinery 2,000 Kilometres Inside Russia Using Drones

media:content.media:description: Ukraine hit a Moscow refinery twice this week

item.description: Ukraine has significantly stepped up attacks on Russian oil infrastructure in recent months, with Russia's crude-processing rates falling to its lowest level in two decades so far in June.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine has significantly stepped up attacks on Russian oil infrastructure in recent months, with Russia's crude-processing rates falling to its lowest level in two decades so far in June.

item.title: Spain PM's Wife To Face Corruption Trial, Told To Surrender Passport

media:content.media:description: Gomez has denied any wrongdoing.

item.description: Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez's wife Begona Gomez is accused of using her position to influence government contracts given to a group of technology companies.
item.content:encoded: Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez's wife Begona Gomez is accused of using her position to influence government contracts given to a group of technology companies.

item.title: "Misanthropy": Millions In Brazil Get Fake Government Mobile Alert, Hack Suspected

media:content.media:description: Representational Image

item.description: Officials said the false messages were "remotely ordered by someone outside the national system of protection and civil defense," and delivered to "various regions of the country."
item.content:encoded: Officials said the false messages were "remotely ordered by someone outside the national system of protection and civil defense," and delivered to "various regions of the country."

item.title: In A First For Monarchs, King Charles To Reveal Personal Tax Bill

media:content.media:description: King Charles will become the first monarch to share his personal tax information

item.description: British monarchs are legally exempt from paying certain taxes, though they have paid some duties on a voluntary basis for decades.
item.content:encoded: British monarchs are legally exempt from paying certain taxes, though they have paid some duties on a voluntary basis for decades.

item.title: Claude Guillemot, Co-founder Of 'Assassin's Creed' Company Ubisoft Killed In Plane Crash

media:content.media:description: Claude Guillemot founded Ubisoft with his four brothers in 1986.

item.description: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of Ubisoft and one of the creators behind the Assassin's Creed and Far Cry franchises, has died at the age of 69 after a small plane crashed in western France.
item.content:encoded: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of Ubisoft and one of the creators behind the Assassin's Creed and Far Cry franchises, has died at the age of 69 after a small plane crashed in western France.

item.title: US-Iran Resume Dialogue Today As Trump Warns Of Tolls On Hormuz If Talks Fail

media:content.media:description: Vance will lead the US team, while Iran's Mohammad Ghalibaf will head the Iranian delegation

item.description: Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday signed a 14-point deal to end the war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday signed a 14-point deal to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Pics: Texas To Times Square, Yoga Day Sees Huge Participation Across US

media:content.media:description: This year's theme, "Yoga for Healthy Ageing," focused on preventive healthcare and mental well-being

item.description: Yoga is practiced by tens of millions of Americans and has become one of the most visible examples of India's cultural influence in the country.
item.content:encoded: Yoga is practiced by tens of millions of Americans and has become one of the most visible examples of India's cultural influence in the country.

item.title: Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz Again Over Israel's Attacks On Lebanon

media:content.media:description: Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz again

item.description: Iran said that the closure was the "first step" in response to what it described as breaches of commitments.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1115909/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Iran said that the closure was the "first step" in response to what it described as breaches of commitments.

item.title: Israeli Army Confirms Journalist Killed In Gaza, Calls Him 'Hamas' Terrorist

media:content.media:description: Israel has carried out a strike in Gaza again.

item.description: The Israeli military said Saturday it had carried out a strike that killed Al Jazeera journalist Ahmed Wishah in Gaza
item.content:encoded: The Israeli military said Saturday it had carried out a strike that killed Al Jazeera journalist Ahmed Wishah in Gaza

item.title: 'Jai Shri Ram' Chants In Dhaka As Thousands Protest 'Desecration' Of Image

media:content.media:description: Thousands of Hindus gathered for a torchlight procession in Dhaka on Friday

item.description: Thousands of Hindus gathered for a torchlight procession in Dhaka on Friday, chanting "Jai Shri Ram" and demanding the arrest of those accused of insulting the image.
item.content:encoded: Thousands of Hindus gathered for a torchlight procession in Dhaka on Friday, chanting "Jai Shri Ram" and demanding the arrest of those accused of insulting the image.
